headline: South China Sea Chinese Vessel Incursions Rise 45 Percent
context: MMEA recorded 45% increase in Chinese vessel incursions into Malaysian-claimed South China Sea waters in 2025.

cards.big: Chinese vessel incursions into Malaysian waters rose 45% in 2025
cards.sub: 280 incidents recorded including 35 involving coast guard or militia ships

cards.big: Luconia Shoals saw 120 days continuous Chinese coast guard presence
cards.sub: Area contains estimated 1.2 trillion cubic feet of natural gas in EEZ

cards.big: MMEA deployed only 8 vessels to patrol 150,000 sq km of contested waters
cards.sub: China South China Sea fleet exceeds 300 vessels dwarfing Malaysian assets

cards.big: Malaysia filed 4 diplomatic protests to Beijing all went unanswered
cards.sub: Government maintains quiet diplomacy while neighbours adopt stronger stances

cards.big: Maritime incursions test whether diplomatic restraint protects sovereignty or concedes it
45% increase in Chinese vessel incursions. South China Sea sovereignty requires presence, not just protests.
